Why Rust Is the Future of Systems Programming

Nora Systems Jun 10, 2024

Rust has been voted the most loved programming language for eight years running. But beyond developer satisfaction, there are concrete technical reasons why Rust is reshaping systems programming.

Memory safety without garbage collection is the killer feature. The borrow checker catches entire categories of bugs at compile time.

Responses (3)

Oscar Perf Jun 10, 2024

The zero-cost abstractions in Rust are incredible. We rewrote our hot path from C++ to Rust and got the same performance with half the bugs.

Petra Embedded Jun 11, 2024

For embedded systems, Rust is a game-changer. No runtime overhead and the type system catches hardware register misuse.

Quinn Skeptic Jun 12, 2024

The learning curve is still steep. Most teams I've seen struggle with lifetimes for the first few months.
